reviews.title: Not a 4 star resort
reviews.text:
This hotel was visually ok, however it did lack the level of service to be called a '4 star' hotel. The restaurant service was extremely slow and it was hard to get the staffs attention to be served. 

Every meal me and my partner had, the food would come out at different times, for example, you would get a plate of vegetables then 10/15 mins later the rice comes out, followed by one main, to which we had to wait another 10 mins for the other main course. Also the riverside restaurant were inflexible at times which disappointed me, as you don't expect this at a 4 star hotel.

reviews.title: Isolated and with minimal resort facilities available.
reviews.text:
The bar, coffee house and games centre were all closed for our entire stay making it a long trek into the city to find something to do and eat for our children. 
The staff offered no ideas or advice in activities to do in the area. 
As the pool was closed due to typhoon Tembin, we found ourselves bound in our room over Xmas. 
The bathroom was not clean (mould on blinds) and there was no tea/coffee in our room as indicated. Supplies in bathroom were not replenished daily. 
For a great dinner a short taxi ride away, head to Thuy Pizza - run by a well known chef. Great hospitality and great food! (Great espresso coffe, too!)

reviews.positives: The room was comfortable, massive bed and a beautiful view from our balcony.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text:
We were really let down by our stay at Ben Tre Riverside Resort. We had paid a premium for a separate villa expecting a higher standard, but it was old, run down, and nowhere near the level advertised. The photos online make it look clean and new however this is not the case.

The spa in our room didn’t work, the door didn’t lock properly and was literally tied shut with a ribbon, which felt extremely unsafe.

Basic service was also very poor. Housekeeping never added any extra toilet paper, reception was unprofessional, taking photos of our Passports on their personal phone. 
The buffet breakfast restaurant staff were genuinely some of the worst we’ve dealt with — plates were never cleared, we had to take them away ourselves, and once food ran out it was never replenished.


The food quality was terrible and the same dishes. Not much variety at all.
Yet we were forced to eat there as there were no convenient alternatives nearby. Service was painfully slow every time.

The resort grounds were also in bad shape with overgrown grass and a very neglected feel overall. The gym and indoor and outdoor kids’ area were extremely old, rundown,  The minibar in the room had no listed prices and we were charged premium rates for items without any warning.

The only positive was that the room itself was clean — the cleaners clearly do a good job — but unfortunately everything else made this stay really disappointing. Definitely not worth the price and we would not return.

reviews.title: The hotel has great potential
reviews.positives: The view of the Mekong River from our room was excellent, and the breakfast had a nice selection of Asian and Western options. We stayed before Tet, too, and the street before the hotel was extremely colorful and lively with flower sellers extending along the riverside for more than five kilometers. The staff were nice and friendly, and one surprised me with his fluency in both English and Japanese. Overall, the hotel was a good deal for its price.
reviews.negatives: It was too bad that the hotel area along the river was often full of trash (a problem in Ben Tre and Vietnam as a whole). It seemed to me that more of an effort could have been made to keep such a stunning attribute of the hotel clean. Also, photos of the hotel on most booking websites do not realistically depict the hotel. The hotel appeared much older and more run-down than we could have possibly known based on the online photos. Finally, the walls of our room were rather thin and you could easily hear other guests in their rooms and especially in the hallways as they opened and closed their doors and talked to each other.

reviews.title: From Good to Bad to Worse
reviews.text: Five years have passed since our first lovely stay at this resort hotel. Since that first wonderful visit, we have returned there many times because we have found the hotel to be most relaxing and enjoyable and the staff to be so accommodating. We have, also, throughout these last 5 years brought many other people to the resort - our own staff and friends and encouraged many others to go there, too. All this goodwill, however, has now ended because during our last 2 visits, we have found the staff to be less than accommodating and unwilling to provide "all" customers staying at the hotel with the same quality service For example, on our most recent visit to the hotel, we (and other visitors) were subjected to (bombarded with) extremely loud noise from a group of youth staying at the hotel (during night rest time. The staff did nothing to curb the problem. on a previous visit, the same occurred. NO, we will NEVER return there nor will many others whom we know.

All the trees are small thus it is quite hot in the noon and the cafe is outdoor, so its very hot under the sun.
The breakfast is terrible!! We went at 8am but all the dishes are cold and they do not top up!!! 
Overall for this kind of price for ben tre area, it is not worth it!! 
I hope they will improve in amenities and facilities to cater things for small kids.

reviews.text:
I stayed for only one day but had enough time to experience and provide the following feedback:
Pros:
- The resort is clean, lush with many trees, beautifully decorated, and has many scenic spots for photos.
- The kitchen staff are enthusiastic, providing guidance and support to guests when needed.
- The swimming pool has clear water and is relaxing.
- The rooms have a nice river view.
- There is acoustic music on the weekends, making it quite peaceful after 9 PM.
- The indoor play area has books for kids to read and some Lego toys.
Cons:
- The resort's design is a bit odd: the back gate is at the front, guests enter through the back gate but have to drive out the back again; the front gate is not accessible due to it being a dead end (which is not actually the case). The dining area is a bit further away from the resting area and the balcony of the rooms is dangerous for climbing children. I hope the resort addresses this issue. The door and bed corner make entry and exit difficult and easy to trip over.
- The river view can occasionally have noisy boats passing by (but it's acceptable).
- Most of the staff are new and not well trained, so they did not know the answers to guest inquiries and did not provide good support.
- The reception staff were not very enthusiastic in guiding guests on how to check in (like where the elevator is and whether to go left or right).
- The zigzag room layout design is a bit hard to find (acceptable).
- The showerhead design is facing the towel rack, which makes it difficult for guests to use.
- The swimming pool has an unexpected drop-off, so there are steps to prevent tripping. Guests should be guided on where to place towels (it's quite hidden, guests don't see it).
- The breakfast buffet has few items and was cold (only pho and fried eggs were heated up immediately, which was okay). The resort should provide warming machines to keep the food hot and tasty for guests.
- Having the café open while the air conditioning is on should limit the door opening, as it ends up making guests sweat and wastes electricity.
- The outdoor playground is suitable for older kids who can climb and move well, around 3-4 years old and up, so younger kids cannot play much (acceptable).
I hope the resort takes these suggestions into account to improve service quality and welcome more guests, as the resort's location is quite good for leisure travel. Best regards!

Thay mặt toàn thể đội ngũ nhân viên, Ben Tre Riverside Resort xin cảm ơn chị đã lựa chọn nghỉ dưỡng cùng Ben Tre Riverside Resort và chân thành cảm ơn chị đã dành thời gian chia sẻ cảm nghĩ về kỳ nghỉ này.

reviews.text:
With not much else in this range to choose from, this resort is worth the stay. It is picturesque, well-located, relaxing, and with gorgeous views of the river. However, the staff's English is often so poor that even the simplest of tasks can be a bit of a challenge. They do make up for it in friendliness and helpfulness though. The most disappointing part was the  restaurant - not only do you have to walk 'next door' via the outside, to get to it, but the food is average at best, with a poor selection for westerners, and very pricey.  Other than drinks, it isn't worth eating here. 

The breakfast was ok but limited, mostly Asian,  with only 1 cereal, no pastries or other western foods to choose from other than bread and omlettes and some of it quite cold (like the bacon).  

The lovely pool area lacks sufficient loungers and chairs, and there is no room service at all. Thus it cannot truly be a 4 star hotel. The basic gym is fine, but without aircon.

reviews.title: Advantages and disadvantages
reviews.text:
I had a 3-day, 2-night stay at the resort, and it isn't my first time here. Overall, the hotel is fine in terms of location, suitable for those who enjoy peace and quiet, far from the center, with a spacious area and a nice green space by the riverside, perfect for strolling or stopping by the café to enjoy the breeze with a cup of afternoon tea. 
The room service and staff were lovely. Breakfast wasn't very diverse and needs improvement, especially for pastries like donuts, croissants, and muffins. 
At night, I couldn't sleep for 2 nights because the guests next door were continuously partying until 1-2 AM with loud music, making it impossible for anyone to sleep; it felt like I was the only one enjoying a party at the hotel while trying to relax. The soundproofing was terrible, resulting in a horrific nighttime experience for the guests. 
The motorcycle parking area still hasn't improved in terms of roofing; during the rainy season, the bikes are wet in the morning and covered in bird droppings and dust on the seat. Every time, I have to bring a cleaning cloth.

reviews.title: Magnifique séjour
reviews.positives:
Nous avons adoré notre séjour au Ben Tre Riverside Resort. L’emplacement au bord de la rivière est superbe, parfait pour se détendre et observer la vie locale. La piscine est agréable et le personnel d’une grande gentillesse.
La propreté est irréprochable — rarement vue à ce niveau. Le petit déjeuner est correct, assez varié pour convenir à tous les goûts. Le restaurant sur place est pratique, surtout que l’hôtel est un peu isolé.
Autre point très appréciable : les excursions en bateau partent directement du quai de l’hôtel. Une très belle expérience, que nous recommandons vivement !

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.positives: J'ai aimé la piscine, l'hôtel au bord du Mékong. Très bon petit déjeuner.

reviews.title: A room with a view
reviews.text: The hotel has some nice features, with rooms offering a lovely view of the river and the swimming pool. The bed was comfortable, and both the bedding and bath towels were of good quality. However, cleanliness was not up to standard — I found a used ear cleaning cotton bud underneath the curtain, which was disappointing. Another downside was the loud karaoke music outside, which made the afternoons far from restful. Parking is also quite limited, so it can be a challenge if you arrive during a busy weekend. Overall, while the hotel has potential, improvements in cleanliness, noise control, and parking are needed.

reviews.text: Everything is clean, my favorite is the swimming pool, the sunset view by the river, delicious breakfast, friendly and enthusiastic staff. One special thing I really like is the bath oil, it seems to be cajeput oil but feels very pleasant, like a spa.
reviews.response: Cảm ơn chị Hà đã lựa chọn Ben Tre Riverside Resort và dành nhiều lời khen cho hồ bơi, bữa sáng cũng như đội ngũ nhân viên. Chúng tôi rất vui khi những chi tiết nhỏ như tinh dầu trong phòng cũng mang lại cho chị cảm giác thư giãn. Rất mong sớm được chào đón chị quay trở lại.

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.positives:
Merci à toutes les équipes du personnel d'avoir fait de notre séjour d'une semaine une semaine idyllique et tout particulièrement au responsable du petit déjeuner qui est d'une gentillesse incroyable.
Tout était absolument parfait le buffet du matin génial, très copieux et varié, la piscine parfaitement propre avec la vue sur la rivière ham luong. Les chambres côté piscine du 2 ème étage sans vis a vis sur les balcons, magnifiques. Et la literie magique de confort et d'espace. La vue sur la rivière de la salle de bain est juste époustouflante... C'est la cinquième fois que nous venons au Vietnam et nous n'avions jamais connu un tel rapport qualité prix, mise à part phu quoc.
Je recommande à 100 % cet hôtel autant en famille qu'en couple.

reviews.title: Beautiful place and surroundings, nice room and views
reviews.text: Great pool, good size and nice big umbrella. The food and service was below average though. Gym is a joke just as with most hotels. Everything fried by default, cold and really bad salads, took ages for food or drinks to arrive, especially at the pool. They just don't clean up the plates or glasses (4 hours long not) we spent over 4million in food and drinks so i can know. Having said that: everyone was super friendly, room was very clean, and i would definitely come here again as most service is pretty bad throughout Vietnam anyway so i wouldn't mind accepting this. Room was better than in the photos. Price per night was reasonable and breakfast was excellent! The best about this hotel were the gardens and views, truly wonderful.

reviews.title: Buon soggiorno con vista
reviews.positives:
La posizione è ottima, anche se è a 4 km dalla zona centrale, qui c’è una vista impareggiabile sul fiume. Abbiamo scelto appositamente una camera con vista e siamo stati molto contenti. La colazione ha alcune opzioni dolci e in generale è stata abbastanza buona e varia. 
C’è una bellissima piscina, ottima per i bambini.
reviews.negatives:
La palestra era davvero trascurata. Mancano manubri e qualche materassino. Gli attrezzi sono datati e cosa peggiore, non c’è aria condizionata ma solo il ventilatore…e fa davvero troppo caldo.
Abbiamo prenotato un tour tra quelli proposti dall’hotel. Il tour è costato 2.200.000 per 3 adulti e un bambino. Non ci ha soddisfatto perché ci siamo accorti che non era il tour concordato e non era un tour di particolare valore. Abbiamo visto la foresta di cocco, visto il villaggio del cacao, l’allevamento delle api (avevamo scelto l’altro tour proprio perché abbiamo paura delle api), navigato sul fiume…in tutte queste attività ci sono state richieste o incoraggiate mance extra oppure le attività erano finalizzate a vendere dei prodotti. Ci hanno fatto mangiare per tutto il tour, ma non era quello che ci aspettavamo…volevamo vedere le attività e la produzione artigianale ma non abbiamo visto in concreto nulla di tutto questo. Non ci interessava mangiare ma conoscere le attività locali. Abbiamo fatto altre escursioni durante il nostro viaggio, questa è stata l’unica ad averci deluso. Il ragazzo che ci ha guidato e’ stato gentile ma la gentilezza non basta per fare un buon tour.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text: Nice big room with balcony overlooking the river which flows past the hotel (ask for river or pool view). A good pool and a basic gym .  Best of all were the staff always a smile and a greeting from Lam and May on the front desk and the restaurant staff , made for a very pleasant stay away from the hustle and bustle of Saigon.. You are a couple of miles away from the centre of Ben Tre , you wont see many western tourists around here  but the locals were very friendly and its a good place to chill out or have a romantic break for a few days

reviews.title: Traveling with our 6 kids
reviews.text: Referred to by taxi drivers as the "coconut hotel" this is a clean and comfortable property.  It's not near much so a taxi ride to get anywhere is necessary.  It's right on the river and our tour guide picked us up at the boat dock which was super convenient.  The rooms are clean.  The pool is as well.  The kids loved it.  We had vouchers for the breakfast buffet which was ok for us.  We are vegan and there weren't many options.  The selection for a non-vegan was wonderful though.  Staff were super helpful and friendly.  We had been traveling for several weeks and had been getting our laundry done every week or so for a very decent price...this hotel though charged by the piece and our laundry bill was $75, a bit of a shock but that was my fault...I didn't read the fine print.  Overall, a great property and very local community.  We didn't see too many travelers in January.

reviews.title: Great  resort on the MeKong River, quiet location great value for money
reviews.text: Ben Tre is a real gem on the MeKong. You are away from the hustle and bustle of the regular MeKong towns of My Tho and Can Tho. The Riverside resort is in a quiet location on edge of town right on the river. The main part of town is easily accessable by bicycle, free to use from the hotel. Each room has a balcony some with river view and some street view. We had the street view, which has a charm of it's own. The breakfast is good, but if there are few guests they have less variety. Some earlier reviews are critical of the staff's English skills, we found that they were just fine. Everything we needed was met by the staff and they were friendly and very helpful.
